A cozy puzzle for calm minds

Beautiful puzzles,
guaranteed solvable.

Slide gorgeous photos back into place at your own pace. No timers shouting at you, no impossible boards — just the quiet satisfaction of things falling into order.

Download for Windows How to play

Free to play · Windows (macOS & Linux coming) · No account needed

Try it — slide the tiles 0 moves · 0:00

Solved!

Get the full game
Move tiles into the empty space

Every shuffle is made of real moves — so this little board, like the game, is always solvable.

The puzzle that can’t trap you

Most sliding puzzles shuffle tiles at random — and almost half of those boards can never be solved. This one scrambles by making real moves from a finished picture, so there is always a way home. Relaxing by design.

100% solvable boards
3 difficulties · 3×3 → 5×5
∞ puzzles from your photos

What’s inside

Made to be a small, daily pleasure

Daily Challenge

One puzzle a day, the same for everyone. The “Daily Shift” keeps weekdays easy and weekends tougher — and your streak grows every day you return.

Your photos, your puzzles

Drop in a favourite photo and crop it right in the game. Family, pets, places you love — endless puzzles that actually mean something.

13 achievements

Speed, mastery, efficiency and a few secrets. Gentle goals that reward you for playing your way — never for grinding.

Themes & reduced motion

Light, dark and high-contrast themes, separate music and sound sliders, and a reduced-motion mode. Comfortable for every player and screen.

Shareable victory cards

Finish a puzzle and the game makes a little keepsake card of your win — your time, your moves, your picture — ready to save and share.

Calm by default

A soft soundtrack and a satisfying tile sound, smooth slides with input that never feels dropped, and progress that saves itself automatically.

A reason to come back

A fresh challenge, every single day

Everyone gets the same puzzle each day, generated from the date so it’s identical on every machine. Keep your streak alive, watch the countdown to the next one, and feel the week’s rhythm — gentle Mondays, a proper Saturday test.

  • Same board for everyone, worldwide
  • Current & best streak, tracked correctly across time zones
  • Difficulty that shifts with the day of the week

Everything you need to know

Game manual

The whole game in a minute — no reading required to start.

  1. 1

    Pick a mode & size

    Choose a Picture puzzle (rebuild a photo) or a Number puzzle, then a grid: 3×3 to ease in, up to 5×5 for a real challenge. Harder sizes unlock as you win.

  2. 2

    Slide the tiles

    Click a tile next to the empty space, or use the arrow keys, to slide it in. Rebuild the picture (or put 1–N in order). The board is always solvable — you can’t get stuck.

  3. 3

    Win, and keep going

    Finish to earn a victory card, unlock the next difficulty, and grow your stats. Come back for the Daily Challenge and keep your streak alive.

Handy to know

  • Stuck? Picture mode has a hint that shows the finished image.
  • Misclick? Undo up to five moves, or reset the board.
  • Make it yours. Add your own photos and crop them in-game.
  • Comfort first. Switch theme, split music/SFX volume, or turn on reduced motion in Settings.

Controls

↑↓←→
Move tiles
Mouse
Click a tile to slide
U
Undo (up to 5)
R
Reset puzzle
P
Pause
M / N
Music / sound
F11
Fullscreen
? / F1
All shortcuts
Esc
Quit (asks first)

Family-crafted

Puzzles with a little soul

The puzzles you see are real photographs — quiet lakes, mountain mornings, a lone pine at golden hour. Add your own and the game becomes a gallery of your memories, one gentle slide at a time.

Play in two minutes

Download & play

Free, no account, nothing uploaded. Windows 10 / 11 (64-bit).

Download for Windows (.zip)

All releases & versions

  • v0.7.0
  • No account
  • Nothing leaves your PC

SHA-256 · SlidingPuzzle-Windows.zip

93c5f18a1651e62fc44cbca2fe5cf09d1ac8020b9c818ae885cbbb6c78a2302d

Optional: confirm the build is genuine. In PowerShell, run Get-FileHash SlidingPuzzle-Windows.zip and check the hash matches the one above. You can also download the .sha256 file.

Is it safe? Why does Windows warn me?

The build isn’t code-signed (certificates are pricey for a free indie game), so Windows SmartScreen shows a blue “unrecognized app” notice the first time you run it. That’s expected — click More info → Run anyway. There’s no installer and no account, and nothing is uploaded: your photos and progress stay on your computer.

  1. 1

    Download & unzip

    Grab the .zip above and extract it anywhere.

  2. 2

    Run the game

    Double-click SlidingPuzzle.exe. First time, Windows SmartScreen may ask — choose More info → Run anyway (it’s an unsigned indie build).

  3. 3

    Play

    That’s it — your progress saves next to the game. No installer, no internet needed.

Still being cared for

What’s new

A few of the most recent updates. See all releases & notes →

  1. v0.7.0 Nov 2025 current
    • New Daily Challenge: the same date-seeded puzzle for everyone, with streaks and a countdown to the next one.
    • The “Daily Shift” keeps weekday puzzles gentle and weekends tougher.
    • Added a reduced-motion mode and made every setting (volume, theme, fullscreen) stick across restarts.
    • Safer auto-saves that can recover a corrupted progress file.
  2. v0.6.0 Nov 2025
    • Undo up to five moves and a confirm-to-reset button.
    • A ? / F1 overlay listing every keyboard shortcut.
    • Built-in screenshots and an FPS counter for the curious.
  3. v0.5.0 Nov 2025
    • A collection gallery that tracks every puzzle you finish.
    • A statistics dashboard with your records, averages and favourites.

Before you download

Questions & answers

The things people ask most. Short version: it’s free, it’s private, and it stays on your PC.

Is it safe to download? Why does Windows warn me?

Yes. The build just isn’t code-signed — signing certificates are expensive for a free indie game — so the first time you run it Windows SmartScreen shows a blue “Windows protected your PC” notice for an unrecognized app. That’s expected: click More info → Run anyway. There’s no installer and nothing is uploaded. If you’d like extra reassurance, the download card shows the file’s SHA-256 so you can verify it’s the genuine build.

Is it really free?

Yes — free to download and free to play, with no ads, no in-app purchases and no account. The game is proprietary (all rights reserved), but it costs you nothing to enjoy it.

Do you collect my data or upload my photos?

No. The game runs entirely on your computer. Your progress, settings, any photos you add and your stats are saved in files next to the game — nothing is sent anywhere and there’s no sign-in. This website uses a privacy-friendly, cookieless analytics counter — it records anonymous page views plus a few aggregate clicks (like the download button), honours Do Not Track and never identifies you. The game itself collects nothing.

Does it work offline?

Completely. Once you’ve unzipped it, no internet is needed — play on a plane, a train or a cabin in the woods. The only thing that needs a connection is checking back here for a new version.

Will there be a Mac or Linux version?

That’s the plan — macOS and Linux builds are on the roadmap for the current Distribution phase. To hear about them first, watch the public repository on GitHub and you’ll see new releases as they land.

How do I add my own photos?

In the game, choose a Picture puzzle and pick Add your own photo. You can crop it right there so it fits the board, then play it at any difficulty. Family, pets, a favourite view — they all become puzzles, and they never leave your computer.

Where does my progress save?

Automatically, in a data folder beside the game — your unlocked levels, best times, achievements, streaks and settings. Writes are atomic and back themselves up, so a crash mid-save won’t lose your progress. Keep that folder if you move the game to a new PC.

Where it’s going

Roadmap

  1. Foundation & progression

    Core gameplay, three difficulties, unlocking and saves.

  2. Polish, audio & achievements

    Music, themes, custom photos, 13 achievements, collection & stats.

  3. Live-ops & player accounts

    Daily Challenge with streaks, victory cards, profiles & guest mode.

  4. Distribution in progress

    Polished builds for Windows, macOS & Linux, and a wider release.

  5. Online & social later

    Global leaderboards and a way to share the day’s board with friends.

Ready for a calmer kind of puzzle?

Slide a few tiles, breathe out, and watch the picture come together.
